paragraphs: 2,2,3,3-Tetrafluoro-3-(trifluoromethoxy)propanoic acid (CASRN: 377-73-1; DTXSID: DTXSID70191136; PubChem CID: 120228; EC Number: 876-098-1) is a perfluoroalkyl ether carboxylic acid (PFECA) with the molecular formula C₄HF₇O₃ and a molecular weight of 230.04 g/mol [1]. As a member of the broader class of per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS), and specifically categorized within the OECD PFAS framework as a perfluoroalkylether carboxylic acid, this compound is characterized by a fully fluorinated carbon backbone bearing an ether-linked trifluoromethoxy substituent and a terminal carboxylic acid functional group [1]. The compound exhibits reactivity with carboxylic groups, amines, and carbonyls, suggesting potential utility as a reactive monomer or chemical intermediate in fluoropolymer synthesis and related fluorochemical manufacturing processes [1]. Based on its structural classification as a PFECA, this chemical is likely encountered in industrial settings associated with the production or processing of fluorinated polymers, fluorosurfactants, or specialty fluorochemicals, consistent with the broader industrial uses documented for structurally analogous PFAS compounds; however, specific manufacturing process roles for CASRN 377-73-1 have not been identified in the available regulatory databases, and this represents a recognized data gap. Similarly, the presence of this compound in specific consumer products has not been documented in the sources available for this assessment. Occupational exposure may plausibly occur among workers engaged in fluorochemical synthesis, polymer processing, or laboratory research involving fluorinated intermediates; residential and general population exposure pathways, as well as environmental contamination routes, remain uncharacterized in the available literature [1].

No occupational exposure limits have been established for 2,2,3,3-tetrafluoro-3-(trifluoromethoxy)propanoic acid by any major regulatory or advisory body. The Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) has not promulgated a permissible exposure limit (PEL) for this substance. The National Institute for Occupational Safety and Health (NIOSH) has not established a recommended exposure limit (REL). The American Conference of Governmental Industrial Hygienists (ACGIH) threshold limit value (TLV) documentation is not publicly accessible without a subscription, and no specific TLV value could be confirmed for this compound at the time of this assessment [3]. The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) has not established a maximum contaminant level (MCL) or maximum contaminant level goal (MCLG) under the Safe Drinking Water Act for this chemical. No EPA Integrated Risk Information System (IRIS) assessment has been completed for CASRN 377-73-1; consequently, no oral reference dose (RfD), inhalation reference concentration (RfC), or cancer classification has been formally derived by EPA IRIS for this substance [3]. The absence of established regulatory limits across all major frameworks reflects the early stage of hazard characterization for this PFECA.

The routes of human exposure to 2,2,3,3-tetrafluoro-3-(trifluoromethoxy)propanoic acid are expected to include inhalation, oral ingestion, and dermal contact, consistent with exposure pathways documented for structurally related PFAS compounds; however, route-specific absorption data for this specific chemical are not available in the sources reviewed for this assessment. No ATSDR Toxicological Profile has been completed for CASRN 377-73-1, and the ATSDR Toxic Substances Portal returned no substantive content for this compound at the time of data collection [2]. In the absence of chemical-specific ADME data, general considerations applicable to short-chain PFECA compounds suggest that carboxylic acid-bearing perfluorinated compounds may be absorbed via the gastrointestinal tract following oral exposure, with distribution potentially influenced by protein binding, particularly to serum albumin and liver fatty acid-binding proteins, as has been characterized for other PFAS [2]. Metabolic transformation of fully fluorinated compounds is generally considered limited due to the exceptional stability of the carbon-fluorine bond; however, the ether linkage in PFECA structures may confer differential metabolic susceptibility relative to linear perfluoroalkyl carboxylic acids. Excretion routes and biological half-lives specific to this compound have not been reported in the available literature, representing a critical gap in the ADME characterization of CASRN 377-73-1 [2].

Toxicological data specific to 2,2,3,3-tetrafluoro-3-(trifluoromethoxy)propanoic acid are not available in the regulatory sources reviewed for this assessment. No acute, subchronic, or chronic toxicity studies were identified for CASRN 377-73-1 in the PubChem compound record, the ATSDR Toxic Substances Portal, or the EPA IRIS database [1, 2, 3]. The ATSDR Toxicological Profile for this compound has not been completed, and the EPA IRIS application returned an error when queried for this substance, indicating the absence of a completed IRIS assessment [2, 3]. In the absence of chemical-specific toxicological data, no target organ toxicity, no-observed-adverse-effect level (NOAEL), lowest-observed-adverse-effect level (LOAEL), or carcinogenicity classification can be reported for this compound. Read-across from structurally related PFECA compounds within the OECD PFAS category may provide provisional hazard information, but such analogues have not been formally identified or evaluated for this assessment. The complete absence of duration-specific toxicological data across acute, subchronic, and chronic exposure windows represents a fundamental limitation in the current hazard characterization of CASRN 377-73-1.

The mechanism of toxicity of 2,2,3,3-tetrafluoro-3-(trifluoromethoxy)propanoic acid has not been characterized in the available regulatory or primary literature. No chemical-specific mechanistic data were retrievable from the PubChem compound record, the ATSDR Toxic Substances Portal, or the EPA IRIS database for CASRN 377-73-1 [1, 2, 3]. Based on its structural classification as a PFECA and its documented reactivity with carboxylic groups, amines, and carbonyls, this compound may interact with biological macromolecules through covalent or non-covalent mechanisms; however, specific molecular targets, receptor interactions, reactive intermediates, or downstream signaling pathway perturbations have not been empirically established for this substance [1]. For structurally related PFAS compounds, proposed mechanisms have included peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor (PPAR) agonism, disruption of lipid and cholesterol homeostasis, interference with thyroid hormone transport, and induction of oxidative stress; whether these mechanisms are operative for CASRN 377-73-1 remains to be determined. The absence of mechanistic toxicology data for this compound precludes mode-of-action analysis and limits the applicability of mechanistic read-across from related PFAS structures.

The hazard characterization and risk assessment of 2,2,3,3-tetrafluoro-3-(trifluoromethoxy)propanoic acid are substantially constrained by pervasive data gaps across all assessment domains. In the exposure characterization domain, the manufacturing process role of this compound is unknown, precluding assessment of occupational exposure in production facilities; consumer product presence has not been documented, preventing characterization of general population exposure; and environmental fate and transport data are absent, making it impossible to assess exposure via contaminated air, drinking water, or soil pathways. In the hazard identification domain, no ATSDR Toxicological Profile has been completed for this substance, and no subchronic or chronic toxicity studies have been identified, leaving intermediate- and long-term health risks entirely uncharacterized; carcinogenic potential has not been evaluated by any authoritative body, and no cancer weight-of-evidence classification exists [2, 3]. In the dose-response assessment domain, no oral RfD, NOAEL, LOAEL, or inhalation RfC has been established, precluding derivation of health-based exposure limits by any route; benchmark dose modeling cannot be performed without a point of departure, and read-across from structurally analogous PFECA compounds has not been formally validated for this substance [3]. In the risk characterization domain, no drinking water MCL or MCLG has been promulgated under the Safe Drinking Water Act, leaving environmental contamination risk unquantified; no occupational exposure limits (OSHA PEL or NIOSH REL) have been established, leaving worker risk assessment without an enforceable or recommended air concentration standard. Of the data elements reviewed, chemical identity parameters (CASRN, DTXSID, molecular structure) and the general industrial/commercial classification within the PFAS/PFECA framework are adequately characterized [1]. In total, 13 of 23 assessed data elements were identified as missing or insufficient, underscoring the early and incomplete state of the toxicological database for CASRN 377-73-1.

Given the classification of 2,2,3,3-tetrafluoro-3-(trifluoromethoxy)propanoic acid as a perfluoroalkyl ether carboxylic acid with potential industrial utility as a reactive fluorochemical monomer, its complete absence of established toxicological, mechanistic, and dose-response data, and the consequent inability to derive health-based exposure limits by any route, this 5-day genomic dose-response study in Sprague-Dawley rats was conducted to characterize chemical-induced gene expression changes, identify molecular targets and perturbed biological pathways, and provide an empirical transcriptomic foundation from which points of departure and mechanistic hypotheses may be developed to support future hazard characterization and risk assessment of this data-poor PFAS compound.
references: [1] National Center for Biotechnology Information. "PubChem Compound Summary for CID 120228, 2,2,3,3-Tetrafluoro-3-(trifluoromethoxy)propanoic acid." PubChem. https://pubchem.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/compound/120228. Accessed 2024.

[2] Agency for Toxic Substances and Disease Registry (ATSDR). "Toxicological Profile for 377-73-1." U.S. Department of Health and Human Services. https://wwwn.cdc.gov/TSP/ToxProfiles/ToxProfiles.aspx?id=&tid=&casrn=377-73-1. Accessed 2024. [Note: No completed ToxProfile was available for this compound at the time of this assessment.]

[3] U.S. Environmental Protection Agency. "IRIS Assessment for CASRN 377-73-1." Integrated Risk Information System. https://cfpub.epa.gov/ncea/iris2/chemicalLanding.cfm?substance_nmbr=377731. Accessed 2024. [Note: No completed IRIS assessment was available for this compound at the time of this assessment.]
